label: Canada / English
ladderNote: Canadian English from Canadian sources (CBC, The Conversation Canada, r/AskACanadian). Closely tracks US/UK Gen Z vocabulary; common-law cohabitation is a distinct Canadian legal/social step. French Canada often uses French terms (petit ami, en couple).

levels.meaning: Mutual attraction or interest, nothing acted on yet.
Showing interest; banter, compliments, light pursuit.
Regular texting / chatting with romantic intent. Discussed on r/AskACanadian and Canadian media.

levels.term: Hanging out / getting to know each other
levels.meaning: Spending time together without a defined label.
Casual sex, often one-off or low-commitment.
Friendship plus recurring sex; usually no romantic label.
Going on dates; may still see other people.
Ongoing romantic/sexual gray zone without clear commitment. Covered by CBC.
Agreed not to date others; the DTR conversation Canadians often name explicitly.
Defined romantic relationship with the label.
Serious couple language; common in Canadian speech.
Cohabiting. Common-law status has real legal meaning in Canada after a period of cohabitation.
